channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: NATS-as-C2: Inside a new technique attackers are using to harvest cloud credentials and AI API keys
item.description: Sysdig Threat Research Team (TRT) has identified what appears to be the first published case of a threat actor using a NATS server as command-and-control (C2) infrastructure — a technique dubbed “NATS-as-C2.” Discover...

item.title: Introducing the updated AWS User Guide to Governance, Risk, and Compliance for Responsible AI Adoption
item.description: The financial services industry (FSI) is using AI to transform how financial institutions serve their customers. AI solutions can help proactively manage portfolios, automatically refinance mortgages when rates decrea...

item.title: Dark Reading Celebrates 20 Years as a Leading Authority on Cybersecurity, Highlighting the People, Events, Ideas, and Technologies Shaping the Modern Risk Landscape
item.description: Informa TechTarget's flagship cybersecurity media brand launches a special content series to mark two decades as a trusted source for cybersecurity professionals.

item.title: OpenLoop Health confirms January 2026 Data breach affecting 716,000
item.description: In January 2026, telehealth infrastructure firm OpenLoop Health suffered a security breach that exposed information of 716,000 people. OpenLoop Health confirmed a January 2026 cyberattack that exposed personal informa...

item.title: Microsoft fixes Windows Autopatch bug installing restricted drivers
item.description: Microsoft has fixed a Windows Autopatch bug that caused driver updates restricted by administrative policies to be deployed on some Autopatch-managed Windows devices in the European Union. [...]

item.title: Microsoft's MDASH AI System Finds 16 Windows Flaws Fixed in Patch Tuesday
item.description: Microsoft has unveiled a new multi-model artificial intelligence (AI)-driven system called MDASH to facilitate vulnerability discovery and remediation at scale, adding that it's being tested by some customers as part...

item.title: Azerbaijani Energy Firm Hit by Repeated Microsoft Exchange Exploitation
item.description: A threat actor with affiliations to China has been linked to a "multi-wave intrusion" targeting an unnamed Azerbaijani oil and gas company between late December 2025 and late February 2026, marking an expansion of its...

item.title: Foxconn confirms cyberattack claimed by Nitrogen ransomware gang
item.description: Foxconn, the world's largest electronics manufacturer, says some of its North American factories are now working to resume normal operations after a cyberattack. [...]

item.title: Most Remediation Programs Never Confirm the Fix Actually Worked
item.description: Security teams have never had better visibility into their environments and never been worse at confirming what they fix stays fixed. Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 report puts the mean time to exploit at an estimated negat...

item.title: Microsoft Patches 138 Vulnerabilities, Including DNS and Netlogon RCE Flaws
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released patches for 138 security vulnerabilities spanning its product portfolio, although none of them have been listed as publicly known or under active attack. Of the 138 flaws, 30 are rated Cr...

item.title: Windows BitLocker zero-day gives access to protected drives, PoC released
item.description: A cybersecurity researcher has published proof-of-concept (PoC) exploits for two unpatched Microsoft Windows vulnerabilities named YellowKey and GreenPlasma, which are a BitLocker bypass and a privilege-escalation fla...

item.title: PCI PIN and P2PE compliance packages for AWS Payment Cryptography are now available
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is pleased to announce the successful completion of Payment Card Industry Personal Identification Number (PCI PIN) and PCI Point-to-Point Encryption (PCI P2PE) assessments for the AWS Payment...

item.title: Mystery Microsoft bug leaker keeps the zero-days coming
item.description: Security pros warn YellowKey claim could make stolen laptops a much bigger problem

item.title: Microsoft, Palo Alto Networks Find Many Vulnerabilities by Using AI on Their Own Code
item.description: Microsoft’s MDASH discovered 16 of the Patch Tuesday vulnerabilities, and Palo Alto used Mythos to find dozens of flaws. The post Microsoft, Palo Alto Networks Find Many Vulnerabilities by Using AI on Their Own Code a...

item.title: Webinar tomorrow: Why security alone won't stop modern attacks
item.description: Tomorrow's webinar examines why prevention alone is no longer enough against modern cyberattacks. The session explores how organizations combine security, backups, and recovery planning to improve cyber resilience aft...

item.title: WhatsApp adds Incognito Chat for private Meta AI conversations
item.description: The company launched Incognito Chat with Meta AI, a feature that lets users hold AI conversations the platform itself cannot read. The rollout will reach WhatsApp and the standalone Meta AI app over the coming months....

item.title: Sweet Security Launches Agentic AI Red Teaming to Counter ‘Mythos Moment’
item.description: New “Sweet Attack” platform uses runtime intelligence and continuous agentic red teaming to identify exploitable attack chains human teams may miss. The post Sweet Security Launches Agentic AI Red Teaming to Counter ‘...

item.title: When IT Support Calls: Dissecting a ModeloRAT Campaign from Teams to Domain Compromise
item.description: Overview Attackers do not need to break into the front door when they can convince employees to open it for them through the tools they already trust. In April 2026, Rapid7 investigated an enterprise intrusion that be...

item.title: Canvas Owner Reaches Agreement With Cybercriminals After Ransomware Attack
item.description: Instructure says it reached an agreement with ShinyHunters over the Canvas breach data

item.title: Chipmaker Patch Tuesday: Intel and AMD Patch 70 Vulnerabilities
item.description: The two chip giants have published over two dozen advisories describing recently identified security defects. The post Chipmaker Patch Tuesday: Intel and AMD Patch 70 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: A 0-click exploit chain for the Pixel 10: When a Door Closes, a Window Opens
item.description: We recently published an exploit chain for the Google Pixel 9 that demonstrated it was possible to go from a zero-click context to root on Android in just two exploits. The Dolby 0-click vulnerability existed across a...

item.title: Juice Shop v20.0.0 — a fresh squeeze of features, now with AI
item.description: Juice Shop v20.0.0 — a fresh squeeze of features, now with AI After months of work on the develop branch, OWASP Juice Shop v20.0.0 is ready to serve. This is a major version bump packed with new challenges, a redesign...

item.title: Rapid7 Partner Academy: Driving Impact with Gold Stevie Award-Winning Partner Services Certifications
item.description: At Rapid7, our commitment to our partners is built on the foundation of the PACT (Partnering with Accountability, Consistency, and Transparency) program. Central to this mission is the Rapid7 Partner Academy, which wa...

item.title: Webinar Today: ROI for Cyber-Physical Security Programs
item.description: This webinar will help OT security teams and asset owners stop being cost centers and start being resilience drivers. The post Webinar Today: ROI for Cyber-Physical Security Programs appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Quest KACE SMA flaw CVE-2025-32975: when one unpatched tool opens the door to 60 organizations
item.description: CVE-2025-32975 is a critical flaw in Quest KACE SMA used for endpoint management. If exploited, it could impact all managed systems across organizations. CVE-2025-32975 is a critical flaw in Quest KACE SMA used for en...

item.title: Signal responds to phishing attacks with new in-app security warnings
item.description: Signal is adding new protections for users following recent phishing and social engineering attacks. In March, the FBI and CISA issued a warning stating that Signal had become a primary target of Russian intelligence-...

item.title: Tuskira’s Kairo exposes hidden AI-driven breach paths
item.description: Tuskira has announced the launch of Kairo, a breach modeling capability that detects deep, hidden breach paths by leveraging its security data mesh and digital twin technology. Kairo helps security teams improve breac...

item.title: Beyond Findings: Connecting Exploitable Risk to Cloud Context with Wiz and HackerOne
item.description: See proven, exploitable risk in the context of your full cloud environment

item.title: Browser Run: now running on Cloudflare Containers, it’s faster and more scalable
item.description: We’ve enabled higher usage limits, faster performance, better reliability, and increased shipping velocity for our Browser Run product by rebuilding on top of Cloudflare’s Containers. Here’s how.

item.title: LatAm Vibe Hackers Generate Custom Hacking Tools on the Fly
item.description: In the latest evolution of automated cyberattacks, two threat campaigns heavily leveraged AI agents to support attacks against entities in Mexico and Brazil.

item.title: Announcing Cloud Security Agent Skills for Orca’s MCP Server
item.description: The gap between security data and security decisions has never been smaller. Cloud security teams are drowning in the right data. They have asset inventories, vulnerability feeds, compliance scores, CloudTrail logs, a...

item.title: Apricorn hardens ASK3 encrypted USB drive for extreme conditions
item.description: Apricorn has announced enhancements to its Aegis Secure Key 3.0 (ASK3), delivering faster performance and new environmental protection capabilities designed to secure the device and its data in the most demanding phys...

item.title: 73 Seconds to Breach, 24 Hours to Patch: The Case for Autonomous Validation
item.description: Attackers can compromise systems in minutes while patching and response still take hours or days. Picus Security breaks down why autonomous validation is becoming critical for modern defense strategies. [...]

item.title: Ransomware: Over Half of CISOs Would Consider Paying Ransom to Hackers
item.description: Survey of cybersecurity leaders suggests that majority would strongly consider paying cybercriminals, if that’s what it took to help restore encrypted systems

item.title: Fragnesia: Linux Kernel Local Privilege Escalation via ESP-in-TCP
item.description: A new page-cache corruption vulnerability in the Dirty Frag family enables unprivileged local attackers to achieve root

item.title: Government to Scrutinize Instructure Over Canvas Disruption, Data Breach
item.description: The Committee on Homeland Security has requested to be briefed on the incident and Instructure’s remediation steps. The post Government to Scrutinize Instructure Over Canvas Disruption, Data Breach appeared first on S...

item.title: [Webinar] Why Your AppSec Tools Miss the "Lethal Path" (and How to Fix It)
item.description: TL;DR: Stop chasing thousands of "toast" alerts. Join experts from Wiz and Okta/GitLab to learn how hackers connect tiny flaws to build a "Lethal Chain" to your data—and how to break it. Register for the Strategic Bri...

item.title: Sandyaa: Open-source autonomous security bug hunter
item.description: Source code auditing has traditionally relied on static analyzers that flag long lists of potential issues, leaving engineers to sort bugs from noise. A new open-source project from offensive-security firm SecureLayer...

item.title: OpenAI’s GPT-5.5 is as Good as Mythos at Finding Security Vulnerabilities
item.description: The UK’s AI Security Institute evaluated GPT-5.5’s ability to find security vulnerabilities, and found that it is comparable to Claude Mythos. Note that the OpenAI model is generally available. Here is the Institute’s...

item.title: KDE gets over €1 million investment to strengthen security and core infrastructure
item.description: European governments and public institutions have been shifting away from proprietary software for years, and the financial infrastructure supporting open-source alternatives is growing to match. Germany’s Sovereign T...

item.title: Global Cyber Agencies Issue New SBOMs for AI Guidance to Tackle AI Supply Chain Risks
item.description: The G7 Cybersecurity Working Group releases new SBOM for AI guidance, outlining seven key data clusters to boost transparency and security across AI supply chains

item.title: Microsoft Patches Critical Zero-Click Outlook Vulnerability Threatening Enterprises
item.description: CVE-2026-40361 is similar to a vulnerability found a decade ago, BadWinmail, which at the time was dubbed an “enterprise killer”. The post Microsoft Patches Critical Zero-Click Outlook Vulnerability Threatening Enterp...

item.title: Instructure settles with hackers following massive student data theft
item.description: Educational tech firm Instructure reached a deal with hackers after a major Canvas breach exposed data stolen from schools and universities. Educational tech firm Instructure says it reached an agreement with the cybe...

item.title: Breaking things to keep them safe with Philippe Laulheret
item.description: Philippe shares his unique journey from French engineering school to the front lines of cybersecurity, explaining how his lifelong love for solving puzzles helps him uncover critical security flaws before they can be...

item.title: Fortinet, Ivanti Patch Critical Vulnerabilities
item.description: Successful exploitation of these flaws could lead to arbitrary code execution and information disclosure. The post Fortinet, Ivanti Patch Critical Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: UK Cybersecurity Market Expands to £14.7bn with Strong Growth in AI Security Firms
item.description: UK cybersecurity sector reaches £14.7bn in revenue, driven by rapid growth in AI security firms, increased investment and rising employment across the industry

item.title: Microsoft Fixes 17 Critical Flaws in May Patch Tuesday
item.description: Microsoft has patched 120 vulnerabilities in this month’s security update round

item.title: GemStuffer Abuses 150+ RubyGems to Exfiltrate Scraped U.K. Council Portal Data
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are calling attention to a new campaign dubbed GemStuffer that has targeted the RubyGems repository with more than 150 gems that use the registry as a data exfiltration channel rather than fo...

item.title: Hundreds of Malicious Packages Force RubyGems to Suspend Registrations
item.description: More than 500 packages were pushed during the attack, but the target appears to have been RubyGems itself rather than users. The post Hundreds of Malicious Packages Force RubyGems to Suspend Registrations appeared fir...

item.title: [GUEST DIARY] Tearing apart website fraud to see how it works., (Wed, May 13th)
item.description: &#;x26;#;x5b;This is a Guest Diary by Joshua Nikolson, an ISC Intern and part of the SANS.edu Bachelor&#;x26;#;39;s degree in Applied Cybersecurity (BACS) program.]

item.title: Critical Fortinet vulnerabilities fixed in FortiSandbox and FortiAuthenticator
item.description: Fortinet patched critical flaws in FortiSandbox and FortiAuthenticator that could let attackers remotely execute code on unpatched systems. Fortinet addressed two critical vulnerabilities affecting FortiSandbox and Fo...

item.title: Malware crew TeamPCP open-sources its Shai-Hulud worm on GitHub
item.description: Where it’s been well and truly forked, seemingly without Microsoft’s code locker noticing

item.title: NetSPI AI-powered Continuous Pentesting identifies high-impact vulnerabilities
item.description: NetSPI launched AI-powered Continuous Pentesting offerings, designed to help organizations continuously identify, validate and reduce risk across dynamic external and cloud environments. Organizations are managing an...

item.title: The hidden risk of non-human identities in AI adoption
item.description: An employee with persistent, unsupervised admin access across critical systems, with no audit trail, no clear owner, and no regular access reviews, would raise immediate concern in most organizations. Yet non-human id...

item.title: Vietnam to develop domestic cloud so it can ditch risky overseas operators for government workloads
item.description: Communist government plans personalized ‘data-driven decision-making based on real-time information’ by 2035

item.title: Android pushes new scam, theft, and AI protections in 2026 update wave
item.description: Phone scammers spoofing bank caller IDs have driven an estimated $980 million in annual losses worldwide, according to Europol. Android’s 2026 security roadmap takes direct aim at that pattern with a verified call sys...

item.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, May 13th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9930, (Wed, May 13th)
Proxying the Unproxyable? Sending EXE traffic to a Proxy, (Wed, May 13th)
item.description: .. if â€œunproxyableâ€ is a word that is ..

item.title: Patch Tuesday - May 2026
item.description: Microsoft is publishing 137 vulnerabilities on May 2026 Patch Tuesday . Microsoft is not aware of exploitation in the wild or public disclosure for any of these vulnerabilities. So far this month, Microsoft has provid...

item.title: Luck isn't a security control: What happened with mini Shai-Hulud and what you need to do
item.description: A new supply chain worm hit 400+ packages. Learn why preventive security, not reactive patching, is the only way to stop the next attack.

item.title: Microsoft releases Windows 10 KB5087544 extended security update
item.description: Microsoft has released the Windows 10 KB5087544 extended security update to fix the May 2026 Patch Tuesday vulnerabilities and resolve an issue with the new Remote Desktop warnings. [...]

item.title: TanStack and 160+ npm/PyPI Packages Compromised in Supply Chain Worm Attack
item.description: Key Takeaways Executive Summary A critical supply chain compromise was disclosed on May 12, 2026, affecting TanStack, Mistral AI, UiPath, and over 160 additional npm and PyPI packages. The attack, attributed to the th...

item.title: Cache-poisoning caper turns TanStack npm packages toxic
item.description: Six-minute supply chain blitz pushed 84 malicious versions with credential theft and disk-wiping code

item.title: New TrickMo Variant Uses TON C2 and SOCKS5 to Create Android Network Pivots
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new version of the TrickMo Android banking trojan that uses The Open Network (TON) for command-and-control (C2). The new variant, observed by ThreatFabric between January and F...

item.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
item.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is...

item.title: OpenAI Launches 'Daybreak' to Help Build Secure By Design Software
item.description: With Daybreak, OpenAI wants its frontier AI models to be used to deploy secure by design software from the ground up

item.title: Exaforce Raises $125 Million for Agentic SOC Platform
item.description: Exaforce has raised a total of $200 million and plans on using the latest investment for product development and international expansion. The post Exaforce Raises $125 Million for Agentic SOC Platform appeared first o...

item.title: Attackers exploit cPanel CVE-2026-41940 to deploy Filemanager Backdoor
item.description: Attackers are exploiting cPanel flaw CVE-2026-41940 to install the Filemanager backdoor and gain unauthorized admin access. Cybercriminals are actively exploiting the critical cPanel vulnerability CVE-2026-41940 (CVSS...

item.title: Hackers accessed BWH Hotels reservation system for months
item.description: BWH Hotels says hackers accessed guest reservation data, including names and contacts, for over six months across multiple hotel brands. BWH Hotels disclosed a data breach, with threat actors having had access to gues...

item.title: Doozy of a Patch Tuesday includes 30 critical Microsoft CVEs
item.description: The good news: no 0-days. The bad news: busy week ahead for Microsoft admins

item.title: Fedora Hummingbird brings the container security model to a Linux host OS
item.description: Container image security pipelines have spent the past several years pushing toward minimal footprints, hermetic builds, and continuous CVE remediation. The Fedora Project is now applying that same approach to the hos...

item.title: It's Patch Tuesday for Microsoft and Not a Zero-Day In Sight
item.description: It's the first time in two years with no zero-days. But with 137 flaws to patch, including nine critical ones, admins still have plenty of work to do.

item.title: Deal Reached With Hackers to Delete Data Stolen From the Canvas Educational Platform
item.description: The company that operates online learning system Canvas said it struck a deal with hackers to delete the data they pilfered in a cyberattack that created chaos for students, many of them in the middle of finals. The p...

item.title: Webinar: Fixing the gaps in network incident response
item.description: IT teams often struggle to quickly coordinate responses across disparate systems during network incidents. This upcoming webinar explores how automation and AI-assisted workflows can reduce response times and help pre...

item.title: Microsoft May 2026 Patch Tuesday, (Tue, May 12th)
item.description: Today&#;x26;#;39;s Microsoft patch Tuesday fixes 137 different vulnerabilities. In addition, the update addresses 137 Chromium-related issues affecting Microsoft Edge.

item.title: Windows 11 KB5089549 & KB5087420 cumulative updates released
item.description: Microsoft has released Windows 11 KB5089549 and KB5087420 cumulative updates for versions 25H2/24H2 and 23H2 to fix security vulnerabilities, bugs, and add new features. [...]

item.title: Microsoft Patches 137 Vulnerabilities
item.description: Fresh security updates resolve critical flaws in Azure, Windows, Dynamics 365, and the SSO Plugin for Jira & Confluence. The post Microsoft Patches 137 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: New Exim BDAT Vulnerability Exposes GnuTLS Builds to Potential Code Execution
item.description: Exim has released security updates to address a severe security issue affecting certain configurations that could enable memory corruption and potential code execution. Exim is an open-source Mail Transfer Agent (MTA)...

item.title: Škoda warns of customer data breach after online shop hack
item.description: Škoda Auto, a wholly owned subsidiary of the Volkswagen Group, has disclosed a data breach after attackers hacked its online shop and stole the personal information of an undisclosed number of customers. [...]

item.title: SAP unveils Autonomous Enterprise for AI-driven business operations
item.description: SAP introduced the Autonomous Enterprise to help enhance the world’s most critical business workflows, so that humans and AI work together to meet the accelerating demands of global business profitably, strategically...

item.title: AI Agents Security for Developers: Don't Let Your Agents Become a Liability
item.description: Using Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, Codex, or another coding agent means giving software access to more than your code. It can also see the credentials available in your workspace, shell, config files, and deve...

item.title: White Circle Raises $11 Million for AI Control Platform
item.description: The startup will invest in accelerating product development, hiring new talent, and expanding its customer base. The post White Circle Raises $11 Million for AI Control Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Enabling AI sovereignty on AWS
item.description: Cloud and AI are transforming industries and societies at unprecedented speed, from accelerating research and enhancing customer experiences to optimizing business processes and enriching public services. At Amazon We...

item.title: Amazon Quick authorization bypass let users reach blocked AI chat agents
item.description: Enterprises running Amazon Quick, the AWS business intelligence and agentic AI service, rely on a feature called custom permissions to restrict who inside an account can use AI chat agents. Fog Security founder Jason...

item.title: Veeam Intelligent ResOps unifies data context and recovery
item.description: Veeam Software announced Veeam Intelligent ResOps, a new solution that unifies data context and recovery operations. As agentic AI accelerates change at machine speed, Intelligent ResOps gives teams the insight they n...

item.title: US bank reports itself after slinging customer data at 'unauthorized AI app'
item.description: Volume and sensitivity of the data cited as chief concerns

item.title: The world’s most “Dangerous” AI, Anthropic’s Mythos, found only one flaw in curl
item.description: Anthropic’s AI found five vulnerabilities in curl, but only one low-severity issue proved to be a real vulnerability. In April, Anthropic made considerable noise announcing Mythos, a new artificial intelligence model...

item.title: Hugging Face Packages Weaponized With a Single File Tweak
item.description: A tokenizer library file present in Hugging Face AI models can be manipulated to hijack the model's outputs and exfiltrate data.

item.title: Free OnlyFans Lure Used to Spread Cross-Platform CRPx0 Malware
item.description: CRPx0 is a complex, stealthy malware campaign that targets macOS and Windows systems, and appears to have Linux capabilities in development. The post Free OnlyFans Lure Used to Spread Cross-Platform CRPx0 Malware appe...

item.title: General Motors to pay $12.75 million over driver data sales
item.description: General Motors has agreed to a $12.75 million settlement with California over allegations that it unlawfully sold drivers’ location and behavioral data to brokers, marking the largest penalty in the history of the sta...

item.title: Introducing Wiz Audit History: Track Every Change Across your Environment
item.description: Wiz Audit History is now GA, providing a continuous, cross-cloud timeline of changes to resource configurations and findings to accelerate incident response and simplify compliance.

item.title: How Rapid7 is bringing Cyber GRC closer to security operations
item.description: Sabeen Malik is VP, Global Government Affairs and Public Policy at Rapid7. ⠀ Security teams need a better way to connect what they detect, what they fix, and what they can prove. The pace of modern security operations...

item.title: Download: The IT and security field guide to AI adoption
item.description: Security and IT teams are under pressure to adopt AI, but many are seeing the opposite of what was promised. Tools that demo well don’t hold up in real workflows. Complexity increases. Trust breaks down. And instead o...

item.title: West Pharmaceutical Services Hit by Disruptive Ransomware Attack
item.description: The company took systems offline globally after hackers exfiltrated data and deployed file-encrypting ransomware. The post West Pharmaceutical Services Hit by Disruptive Ransomware Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Apple Patches Dozens of Vulnerabilities in macOS, iOS
item.description: The tech giant has also ported the patch for a recent deleted chats recovery issue to older versions of iOS. The post Apple Patches Dozens of Vulnerabilities in macOS, iOS appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: JetBrains TeamCity vulnerability allows privilege escalation, API exposure (CVE-2026-44413)
item.description: JetBrains has patched a high-severity vulnerability (CVE-2026-44413) in TeamCity, its popular continuous integration and continuous delivery platform, and is urging organizations with on-premises and self-managed depl...

item.title: 20 Leaders Who Built the CISO Era: 2 Decades of Change
item.description: As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary special coverage, we profile the CISOs, founders, researchers, criminals, and policymakers who rewrote the enterprise risk playbook.

item.title: Attackers Combine ClickFix With PySoxy Proxying to Maintain Persistence
item.description: Exploitation of open-source tools allows attackers to maintain persistent access after initial social engineering, warn ReliaQuest researchers

item.title: Webinar: What the Riskiest SOC Alerts Go Unanswered - and How Radiant Security Can Help
item.description: Why do the Riskiest SOC Alerts Go Unanswered? Security operations teams are drowning in alerts. But the real problem isn't always alert volume; it's the blind spots. The most dangerous alerts are the ones no one is in...

item.title: Citrix moves secure access to a flexible, credit-based consumption model
item.description: Citrix has introduced Citrix Platform Flex, a secure access platform that combines software, management, and infrastructure to deliver managed desktops, enterprise browsing, and zero-trust access in a single offering....

item.title: Claude Mythos Finds Only One Curl Vulnerability; Experts Divided on What It Really Means
item.description: Curl’s lead developer says Mythos claims are marketing, but many in the industry believe the results stem from Curl’s robust security. The post Claude Mythos Finds Only One Curl Vulnerability; Experts Divided on What...

item.title: Copy.Fail Linux Vulnerability
item.description: This is the worst Linux vulnerability in years. TL;DR copy.fail is a Linux kernel local privilege escalation, not a browser or clipboard attack. Disclosed by Theori on 29 April 2026 with a working PoC. It abuses the k...

item.title: SAP fixes critical vulnerabilities in Commerce Cloud and S/4HANA
item.description: SAP has released the May 2026 security updates addressing 15 vulnerabilities across multiple products, including two critical flaws in the Commerce Cloud enterprise-grade e-commerce platform and the S/4HANA ERP suite....

item.title: Is The SOC Obsolete, And We Just Haven’t Admitted It Yet?
item.description: Many AI-first enterprises have already embraced sovereign architectures for general AI initiatives; cybersecurity—and the SOC—should be next. The post Is The SOC Obsolete, And We Just Haven’t Admitted It Yet? appeared...

item.title: Six new dnsmasq vulnerabilities open the door to DNS cache poisoning, local root
item.description: Recent disclosures have revealed that open-source networking tool dnsmasq is grappling with a serious set of vulnerabilities. The problems span memory safety and input validation, with researchers identifying heap buf...

item.title: Why Agentic AI Is Security's Next Blind Spot
item.description: Agentic AI is already running in production environments across many organizations today. It is executing tasks, consuming data, and taking actions — most likely without meaningful involvement from the security team....

item.title: State-sponsored actors, better known as the friends you don’t want
item.description: Responding to a state-sponsored threat is nothing like responding to ransomware, and the differences can make or break the outcome. Learn why your IR plan might need revisiting, and the factors you should consider.

item.title: WannaCry, the ransomware attack that changed the history of cybersecurity
item.description: WannaCry showed how unpatched flaws and leaked cyber tools can cripple global systems, reshaping cybersecurity defenses worldwide. In memory of the day the digital world was shaken, but learned to fight back. The Wann...

item.title: Malicious Hugging Face Repository Typosquats OpenAI
item.description: HiddenLayer reveals infostealer malware in a Hugging Face repository

item.title: South Staffordshire Water Fined £1m After Data Breach
item.description: The ICO has fined South Staffordshire Water nearly £1m for a series of data protection failings

item.title: Japan’s PM orders cybersecurity review to stop Mythos going full CyberZilla
item.description: Fears exponential increase in attack scale and speed

item.title: The hidden smart fridge risks that emerge years after purchase
item.description: Household refrigerators are built to last more than a decade. The software, cloud services, and mobile apps that control them are not. A new analysis from Erik Buchmann at Leipzig University maps what happens when tho...

item.title: Elastic Security MCP App: Interactive security operations inside your AI Tools
item.description: Elastic Security is the first security vendor to ship an interactive UI in AI tools. Triage alerts, hunt threats, correlate attack chains, and open cases, all from inside your AI conversation.

item.title: Introducing Prempti: Runtime security for AI coding agents, powered by Falco
item.description: AI coding agents like Claude Code and Codex have become a natural part of the developer workflow. You give them a task and they read your files, run commands, make network requests, and write code, all on your behalf...

item.title: CVE-2026-44338: PraisonAI authentication bypass in under 4 hours and the growing trend of rapid exploitation
Double Canvas breach acknowledged as ShinyHunters sets new pay-or-leak deadline
item.description: UPDATED: Sorry, kids, everything's back up so get to work on your new assignment - An essay on the ethics of paying ransoms, because it looks like that's what happened here

item.title: Apple Patches Everything, (Mon, May 11th)
item.description: Apple today released its typical feature update across it&#;x26;#;39;s operating systems (iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, watchOS, vision OS). With this update, Apple patched 84 different vulnerabilities. Updates are availa...

item.title: Inside AD CS Escalation: Unpacking Advanced Misuse Techniques and Tools
item.description: Unit 42 analyzes AD CS exploitation through template misconfigurations and shadow credential misuse while offering behavioral detection for defenders. The post Inside AD CS Escalation: Unpacking Advanced Misuse Techni...

item.title: Tech Can't Stop These Threats — Your People Can
item.description: Security controls can do only so much. Here are four attacks where your employees are usually your first, and only, line of cyber defense.

item.title: Identity security firm SailPoint discloses GitHub repository breach
item.description: SailPoint disclosed a GitHub repository breach on April 20. The company contained the incident and said no customer data was affected. SailPoint is a cybersecurity company that provides identity security and identity...
